message:
refactor(pager): replace sharded RwLock overflow plane with DashMap; add S3-FIFO eviction policy

Two pager improvements that land together because they share cache
infrastructure changes.

(1) Replace the 64-shard RwLock<HashMap> overflow publication plane
    with a lock-free DashMap, eliminating the publish-side
    serialization that was the remaining bottleneck under concurrent
    write workloads above ~32 threads.  The old shard fan-out was a
    mitigation for global lock contention; DashMap gives us
    per-bucket fine-grained locking natively.

(2) Introduce a configurable PageCacheEvictionPolicy enum with an
    Arbitrary default (preserves current behavior) and an
    S3Fifo(S3FifoConfig) variant that reconstructs an S3-FIFO
    queue state from recent accesses and uses it for victim
    selection.  This is exposed as a public API on PageCache and
    ShardedPageCache so downstream configuration can opt into the
    new policy without touching internals.

crates/fsqlite-pager/Cargo.toml
- Add dashmap.workspace = true to [dependencies].

crates/fsqlite-pager/src/lib.rs
- Export PageCacheEvictionPolicy alongside the existing PageCache /
  ShardedPageCache / PageCacheMetricsSnapshot / DEFAULT_PAGE_BUFFER_MAX /
  resolve_page_buffer_max re-exports.

crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
- Import dashmap::DashMap.
- Delete the sharded overflow plane constants that are no longer
  referenced:
    PUBLISHED_PAGES_SHARD_COUNT (64)
    PUBLISHED_PAGES_SHARD_MASK
    PUBLISHED_PAGES_GOLDEN_RATIO
- Rename ShardedPublishedPages → ConcurrentPublishedPages and
  replace its internals:
    Before: Box<[RwLock<HashMap<PageNumber, PageData, foldhash>>; 64]>
            + a multiplicative-hash shard selector.
    After:  DashMap<PageNumber, PageData, foldhash::fast::FixedState>.
- Simplify get(), insert(), remove(), len(), iter_snapshot() to
  delegate directly to DashMap.  The page_count: AtomicUsize is
  retained because some call sites need a racy approximate count
  without locking the map.
- Drop Self::shard_index() — no longer meaningful.
- All previous call sites in the pager that walked .shards[] for
  diagnostics now iterate the DashMap directly via .iter().
- The surrounding RwLock-based invariants (e.g. "holding the
  publish lock serializes page N's visibility") are preserved
  because DashMap's per-bucket locks still establish a
  happens-before edge between insert and subsequent get.

crates/fsqlite-pager/src/page_cache.rs
- New public enum PageCacheEvictionPolicy {
      #[default] Arbitrary,
      S3Fifo(S3FifoConfig),
  }.
- New private S3FifoQueueSnapshot { small_len, main_len, ghost_len,
  small_capacity } + S3FifoEvictionTracker { config,
  adaptation_interval, adaptive_bounds, access_trace: VecDeque,
  max_trace_entries } that replay recent accesses into an S3-FIFO
  probe and carry the reconstructed state forward.
- record_access() pushes page numbers into the bounded access trace
  (sized to 8× the configured capacity, floor 64).
- On eviction, the tracker is replayed into a fresh S3Fifo probe
  which picks the victim consistent with the policy; this is the
  "reconstruct from trace" approach that lets the eviction logic
  stay stateless in the hot publish path and only pay the S3-FIFO
  cost at the actual eviction site.
- Module-level docs updated: "partitions the page-number space
  across 128 shards, each protected by its own mutex" → "across a
  power-of-two shard array, each protected by its own mutex" so
  future reshards don't invalidate the doc.
- Imports: std::cell::{Cell, RefCell}, std::collections::{HashSet,
  VecDeque}; pull S3-FIFO types from crate::s3_fifo.
- New unit tests (in the existing #[cfg(test)] mod block) exercise
  the S3FifoEvictionTracker under increasing capacity and verify
  that the replayed victim matches what a fresh S3Fifo would
  choose given the same access sequence.

No behavior change when Arbitrary (the default) eviction policy is
selected.  Callers wanting S3-FIFO must explicitly construct
PageCacheEvictionPolicy::S3Fifo(S3FifoConfig::...).  DashMap
migration is a pure throughput improvement; the public
ConcurrentPublishedPages API is source-compatible with what the
old ShardedPublishedPages exposed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
